author: @ErickWhite-Gronok
text:
Jeremy, I've never had a job that gave me PTO. Well, I can say twice, but both places found a way to screw me out of getting it every year. I am 49 tomorrow, (the 4rth) and I have never taken a vacation, or even had time off that I didn't have to worry about "Well they won't pay me, so I can't pay my bills" or "Crap, weather kept me from doing landscaping or window washing or construction/excavation for a few days this week, I guess that means I'm not eating to make sure my family does and my bills get paid. I'm a hard working country boy that's also worked at the house, and around town, and took care of my disabled grandparents for 22 years while ... apparently having more health issues that I knew that's just caught up for me. 

Now, I'm not insulting your work. I went to school to be a computer tech and network engineer, but I took what jobs I could around here when my disabled Vietnam vet Grandpa got bad, and then my grandma after that. I was their full time care giver. Most relationships vanished as women didn't want to share my time with responsibility.... but it is what it is. What I am saying is, I understand not having paid time off and having to work.
